name: Team Fair Fight
subject: Power cedes nothing without a demand
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fair Fight Action
body:
|
---
[](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa697/34a064cc/62b6aed/703806c/2540306610/VEsH/ "https://www.fairfightgeorgia.com/")

| | | Friend - We live in a nation that spent centuries denying the right to vote to the poor, to women, and to people of color. Voter suppression used to be quite plain: the denial of the right to vote was written into law. But all across the country, activists, organizers, and ordinary citizens joined together on the front lines of our democracy and chose action in the face of adversity. Because they knew that power cedes nothing without a demand. Bit by bit and day by day, ordinary people worked to achieve something extraordinary. Congressman John Lewis, who began his career in civil service as an organizer and ultimately led the Voter Education Project, became one of our most celebrated civil rights heroes. Ardies Mauldin, a fifty-two year old nurse who twice tried to register to vote in Dallas County, Alabama, became the first voter registered under the Voting Rights Act of 1965. Today, we carry on this legacy, working to ensure every eligible voter can register and access the ballot. [**Will you make a contribution to Fair Fight tonight to show that you're ready to keep fighting for a future where every voter 's voice can be heard?**](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa697/34a064cc/62b6aed/703806d/2540306610/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fairfightga2018-2?refcode=IE042219HERO") _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ **[Chip in $5 NOW](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa697/34a064cc/62b6aed/703806a/2540306610/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fairfightga2018-2?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=IE042219HEROexpress_lane_5")** **[CHIP IN $18 NOW](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa697/34a064cc/62b6aed/703806b/2540306610/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fairfightga2018-2?express_lane=true&amount=18&refcode=IE042219HEROexpress_lane_18")** **[CHIP IN $25 NOW](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa697/34a064cc/62b6aed/7038068/2540306610/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fairfightga2018-2?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=IE042219HEROexpress_lane_25")** **[CHIP IN $40 NOW](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa697/34a064cc/62b6aed/7038069/2540306610/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fairfightga2018-2?express_lane=true&amount=40&refcode=IE042219HEROexpress_lane_40")** The Voting Rights Act of 1965 forced architects of voter suppression to embark on a decades-long quest to create new barriers to prevent historically disenfranchised communities from exercising their constitutional right. All across the country, politicians put in place obstacles and barriers that on their face seemed fairly low bars to overcome. But when they came together, they formed a system intent on voter suppression and undermined our constitutional right to vote. Yet, voting rights heroes like Fannie Lou Hamer, Modesto Rodriguez, and James Meredith risked everything to deliver a representative government and access to the ballot. They knew that the right to vote was sacred, the fount from which all of our rights flowed. All across our country, the right to vote is under attack. Too much is at stake to let up now. [**Will you make a contribution tonight and join us as we come together to demand free and fair elections for all?**](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa697/34a064cc/62b6aed/703806d/2540306610/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fairfightga2018-2?refcode=IE042219HERO") Onward,
Team Fair Fight

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Original message from Kamala Harris, April 22, 2019

I need to be direct --
I’m reaching out because we’re at a real risk of falling short of our
April fundraising goals. I hope you’ll give me a moment to explain what
these monthly fundraising goals mean.

But first, I have to ask: will you
pitch in your first donation to our campaign for president? We are
rejecting contributions from corporate PACs and lobbyists -- putting our
campaign directly in the hands of our supporters. We need 10,000
first-time donors to step up with their first donation of any amount
before April is over:

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation will go through immediately:

Over the past few days, I have been campaigning in South Carolina.
Tonight, I’m in New Hampshire for a nationally televised CNN town hall --
and then I’ll be heading out again to travel across the country to hear
directly from supporters why they are excited about joining our
people-powered campaign.

As always -- maybe most importantly -- I am looking forward to answering
questions about my vision for our country, and hearing more about the
issues that matter most to your communities.

I am excited to report that our campaign team is also growing with each
passing day. We are staffing up at our headquarters, building out
departments to run the most successful campaign we can. And we are hiring
diverse staff across the country to begin outreach, organizing on the
ground to build our movement -- a true grassroots-up model.

This is how we are going to win: putting the power in the hands of the
people who are powering our campaign. People just like you.

The plans I listed above are just some of the amazing things on the
horizon for our team -- things that YOU made possible.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ema-190422-eom?refcode=ema190422-eom3-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amount=5&t=1013&refcode2=4433_5755789_jmoId-&akid=4433%2E5755789%2EjmoId- ]This is exactly why we cannot afford to fall short of our monthly
fundraising goals. I wouldn’t ask if it didn’t mean so much: can you help
us get back on track for April with
your very first
donation today?

Thank you for your help,

— Kamala Harris

name: Team Ed Markey
subject: Mueller must testify → SIGN THE PETITION
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Markey Committee
body:
|
---
[]()

| | | Team - Ed is calling on Congress to have Robert Mueller testify immediately to discuss the alarming conduct by President Trump.

_**[President Trump may have obstructed justice, and the American people are depending on Congress to act. Sign our petition and demand Congress have Mueller testify.](http://ma.edmarkey. "https://ma.edmarkey.com/page/s/demand-mueller-testify?source=042319&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")**_

Begin Forward from Ed Markey:
* * *
**URGENT ACTION NEEDED:** [_**Sign the petition to demand Congress act immediately to have Robert Mueller testify and explain his report to the American people.**_](http://ma.edmarkey. "https://ma.edmarkey.com/page/s/demand-mueller-testify?source=042319&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=") Team,

Special Counsel Robert Mueller's report paints a deeply disturbing picture of President Trump, his 2016 campaign, and his administration.

The report details multiple instances and a host of evidence that President Trump may have obstructed justice. Any claims by the President or his acolytes that this report exonerates him are untrue.

Congress must investigate the conduct outlined in Mueller's report. We need to hear from the Special Counsel himself.

[_**Sign my petition and demand Congress act immediately to have Robert Mueller testify and explain his report to the American people.**_](http://ma.edmarkey. "https://ma.edmarkey.com/page/s/demand-mueller-testify?source=042319&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

**[SIGN THE PETITION](http://ma.edmarkey. "https://ma.edmarkey.com/page/s/demand-mueller-testify?source=042319&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")**
We cannot rely on the words of Attorney General Barr about the seriousness of President Trump's actions. The Attorney General is clearly acting more as the personal lawyer of the president than the top lawyer for the American people.

The alarming allegations in Mueller's report demand an investigation to determine what actions, if any, should be taken.

We need public pressure to ensure Congress, especially Republicans, do their jobs.

[_**Sign my petition and demand Congress act immediately to have Robert Mueller testify about these serious allegations.**_](http://ma.edmarkey. "https://ma.edmarkey.com/page/s/demand-mueller-testify?source=042319&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

Thanks for standing with me to ensure we hold President Trump accountable,

Ed

subject: No “tolerance” for Pence?
body:
| | Peter, Over the past few weeks, Vice President Pence has, once again, been on the receiving end of attacks because of his sincerely held Christian beliefs. South Bend Indiana Mayor, Pete Buttigieg, attacked Pence for his Christian beliefs on marriage, and shortly after, more than 4,000 people signed a petition urging Taylor University, an evangelical school, to cancel Mike Pence as their commencement speaker. **What is Mike Pence’s public crime? Believing in the biblical definition of marriage and religious freedom for all.** While we see an aggressive movement claiming to champion tolerance and diversity, it seems they only extend it to people with whom they agree, leaving attacks on Christians as fair game. Vice President Pence said, “I hope that Pete will offer more to the American people than attacks on my Christian faith or attacks on the president as he seeks the highest office in the land." Pence went on to say, “I think Pete's quarrel is with the First Amendment. All of us in this country have the right to our religious beliefs." **These recent events are so much more than merely the latest political attacks on our Vice President, it’s about an onslaught of attacks on freedom of speech and freedom of conscience in our nation.** If Americans fail to understand the entire value and meaning of religious freedom, protected by the First Amendment, then the very principle that birthed America and set her apart from the rest of the countries in the world, will be lost. This is why it’s so vital for us to stand in support of Vice President Pence and share with other Christians why the influence of our faith must be brought into the voting booth and our communities. **I want to encourage you to[send a message of support to Vice President Pence’s office](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=rn9Y4Wi5l_dTjWG5qMot1BwfuYh1AmmIQrwRAQzWYDA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190423p2a01&n=1). He needs to hear from the millions of Christians who appreciate the bold stand he has taken for faith.** [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=rn9Y4Wi5l_dTjWG5qMot1BwfuYh1AmmIQrwRAQzWYDA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190423p2a01&n=2) **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190423p2a01&n=3)
---

name: Kamala Harris
subject: If Congress fails to act, I will take executive action to keep our kids and communities safe from gun violence
body:
Content warning: this email contains references to gun violence that some
readers may find upsetting.

Columbine High School.
Virginia Tech.
Sandy Hook Elementary.
Mother Emanuel AME Church.
Pulse.
Las Vegas.
Parkland.
Tree of Life.

Each of these places ought to be known for their joy, enlightenment,
worship, and togetherness -- not for gut-wrenching tragedies after
senseless gun violence.

In the days, months, and years since these horrifying moments, as a
country, we watched as countless lives were taken by mass shootings and
everyday gun violence that ripped through our communities.

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/kdh-email-petition-190423-gun-violence-plan?refcode=ema190423-Full&source=ema190423-Full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&t=1001&akid=4434%2E5755789%2EfLLAfP ]Enough. We don’t need any more tragedies. We need direct action. We
need leaders who have the courage to speak up. If you’re ready to do
something right now to curb gun violence, sign my pledge to join the
fight.

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/kdh-email-petition-190423-gun-violence-plan?refcode=ema190423-Full&source=ema190423-Full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&t=1002&akid=4434%2E5755789%2EfLLAfP ]SIGN THE PETITION

I believe no one should
feel the tremendous pain of losing a loved one from gun violence. Most of
us can agree with that. So, what is stopping us as a country from
listening to our conscience and fighting for what’s morally right?

Here’s the truth: we
aren’t waiting on solutions to end this violence -- the good ideas already
exist. But for far too long, we’ve seen the NRA and their backers in
Congress put politics over people’s lives.

I won’t allow them to stand in the way of curbing this epidemic any
longer. For months, the NRA and their base have repeatedly attacked our
campaign for amplifying the truth about gun violence and the corporate gun
lobby. These attacks will only continue, but I choose to lead with courage
and refuse to back down from speaking the truth.

That’s why I’m proud to announce the following executive actions on gun
violence if Congress fails to act in the first 100 days of my presidency.
Here’s our plan:

* Mandate near-universal background checks by requiring anyone who sells
five or more guns per year to run a background check on all gun sales.
* Revoke the licenses of gun manufacturers and dealers that break the
law and take the most egregious offenders to court.
* Reverse President Trump’s dangerous change to the definition of
“fugitive from justice,” which has allowed thousands of fugitives with
outstanding arrest warrants to buy guns.
* Close the “boyfriend loophole” to prevent dating partners convicted of
domestic abuse from buying guns.

Some will say this is “too bold.” But if Congress continues to bury their
heads in the sand, our only choice is to take bold action.

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/kdh-email-petition-190423-gun-violence-plan?refcode=ema190423-Full&source=ema190423-Full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&t=1003&akid=4434%2E5755789%2EfLLAfP ]That’s where you come in. This is too important to fight alone --
gun violence is everyone’s business in this country. I need you with me
here. Add your voice to say enough is enough.

For The People,

— Kamala Harris

Kamala Harris is not afraid to take on the powerful gun lobby. In her
first 100 days as president, if Congress fails to send comprehensive gun
safety legislation to her desk, she will take executive action to keep our
communities safe.

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/go/9381?t=1004&akid=4434%2E5755789%2EfLLAfP ]Read Kamala’s gun safety plan today and share it with your family and
friends.

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/go/9381?t=1005&akid=4434%2E5755789%2EfLLAfP ]READ THE PLAN

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://action.kamalaharris.org/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=4434%2E5755789%2EfLLAfP

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: White House Easter Egg Roll Rolls on for 141st Year
body:
“The 141st White House Easter Egg Roll – and first lady Melania Trump's third – got underway on the South Lawn Monday.” | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9f6ac2a2e1&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **White House Easter Egg Roll Rolls on for 141st Year**

“The 141st White House Easter Egg Roll – and first lady Melania Trump's third – got underway on the South Lawn Monday,” Maria Puente writes for _USA Today_.

“More than 30,000 people were expected to join President Trump for the annual rewind of one of the oldest White House traditions, aimed mostly at kids. The main event is the spectacle of children rolling hard-boiled eggs across the lawn, but the first lady introduced two new additions to the festivities: musical eggs and a game of hopscotch named for her ‘Be Best’ initiative to improve the well-being of American children.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=92eb64bc87&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “It has been clear from the start that the collusion probe has been a stand-in for policy; for the past two years it has been so much easier to attack President Trump on bogus charges of working with the Russians than to actually propose better ideas for moving the country forward,” Liz Peek writes in [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=87183ef5bc&e=3ee4985eea). “Democrats face two problems. First, the country is increasingly crediting President Trump with buoyant job creation, higher productivity and wage gains. Second, their party is cracking apart, suffering a leadership vacuum and a surfeit of dissonant noise-makers hungry for attention.”
---
|
---
| “President Trump wants to exert ‘maximum pressure’ on Iran, which is why he is giving the sanctions screws another firm twist. Any country that imports Iranian oil will soon face U.S. penalties—with no exceptions,” _[The Wall Street Journal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ce7f140117&e=3ee4985eea)_ editorial board writes. “The Trump Administration, to its credit, shows no signs of backing down. If Iran wants sanctions eased, it can stop spreading terror and renegotiate the nuclear deal.”
---
|
---
| “Last week, Sen. Bernie Sanders introduced a bill that would end health care as we know it, and Americans should thank him for letting us see where the Democrats want to take the country. Its central premise appears to be that Washington bureaucrats know better than patients and doctors,” Director of the White House’s Domestic Policy Council Joe Grogan writes in _[RealClearPolitics](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e49e5c2664&e=3ee4985eea)_. There are real problems to fix with U.S. healthcare, but the solutions should not include fewer choices for American patients.
---
|
---
| “Hamas professes violence and the destruction of Israel as a method of gaining a better life for Palestinians. This ‘defense’ of Palestinians has led to the problems experienced today: a decimated economy, hundreds killed in violence each year and one of the highest unemployment rates in the world,” U.S. Special Representative for International Negotiations Jason Greenblatt writes in _[The New York Times](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=af3ac77158&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Hamas is to blame for Gaza’s situation.”

clean_body: “The 141st White House Easter Egg Roll – and first lady Melania Trump's third – got underway on the South Lawn Monday.” | | | | | | | White House Easter Egg Roll Rolls on for 141st Year “The 141st White House Easter Egg Roll – and first lady Melania Trump's third – got underway on the South Lawn Monday,” Maria Puente writes for USA Today. “More than 30,000 people were expected to join President Trump for the annual rewind of one of the oldest White House traditions, aimed mostly at kids. The main event is the spectacle of children rolling hard-boiled eggs across the lawn, but the first lady introduced two new additions to the festivities: musical eggs and a game of hopscotch named for her ‘Be Best’ initiative to improve the well-being of American children.” | | “It has been clear from the start that the collusion probe has been a stand-in for policy; for the past two years it has been so much easier to attack President Trump on bogus charges of working with the Russians than to actually propose better ideas for moving the country forward,” Liz Peek writes in . “Democrats face two problems. First, the country is increasingly crediting President Trump with buoyant job creation, higher productivity and wage gains. Second, their party is cracking apart, suffering a leadership vacuum and a surfeit of dissonant noise-makers hungry for attention.” | | “President Trump wants to exert ‘maximum pressure’ on Iran, which is why he is giving the sanctions screws another firm twist. Any country that imports Iranian oil will soon face U.S. penalties—with no exceptions,” editorial board writes. “The Trump Administration, to its credit, shows no signs of backing down. If Iran wants sanctions eased, it can stop spreading terror and renegotiate the nuclear deal.” | | “Last week, Sen. Bernie Sanders introduced a bill that would end health care as we know it, and Americans should thank him for letting us see where the Democrats want to take the country. Its central premise appears to be that Washington bureaucrats know better than patients and doctors,” Director of the White House’s Domestic Policy Council Joe Grogan writes in . There are real problems to fix with U.S. healthcare, but the solutions should not include fewer choices for American patients. | | “Hamas professes violence and the destruction of Israel as a method of gaining a better life for Palestinians. This ‘defense’ of Palestinians has led to the problems experienced today: a decimated economy, hundreds killed in violence each year and one of the highest unemployment rates in the world,” U.S. Special Representative for International Negotiations Jason Greenblatt writes in . “Hamas is to blame for Gaza’s situation.” | | | | | | | The White House 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111

| | I won't take up too much of your time. DNC rules say my son, Julian needs 65,000 contributions to participate in the Democratic primary debates -- _he 's almost there! _ **I see you haven 't yet made a contribution, so I wanted to reach out and ask you personally:** [_**Is there any way you could chip in $12, or any amount, before midnight to push him over the top? It would mean so much to see my son up on that stage. >>**_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcd40/3eb3e2fc/4c0ba1f0/7449f7e1/630288389/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rosie-castro?refcode=20190423_em_fr_b1_rosie_nd&recur_weekly=on&recurring=on&amount=25") -Rosie \-- Original Message -- As a single mother, I know every dollar is precious to your family. So I wouldn't ask unless I truly needed your help. [**_My son, Juli an only needs 10,000 more contributions to meet the DNC's criteria to participate in the Democratic debates. Could you chip in any amount before midnight to make sure he's on that stage? >>_**](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcd40/3eb3e2fc/4c0ba1f0/7449f7e1/630288389/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rosie-castro?refcode=20190423_em_fr_b1_rosie_nd&recur_weekly=on&recurring=on&amount=25") I know Julian isn't a frontrunner in this Presidential race. I know the media doesn't give him as much coverage as other candidates. But our family has never been frontrunners. We've never had anything handed to us. I taught Julian to work hard for everything he's ever earned. **And seeing his hard work pay off these last few weeks makes me so incredibly proud --_I can 't believe how close he's come to reaching that stage!_** So I'm humbly asking: Will you chip in before midnight so we can see my son share his story and vision on that Democratic debate stage? | | [**Chip in $10 now >> **](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcd40/3eb3e2fc/4c0ba1f0/7449f7e0/630288389/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rosie-castro?refcode=20190423_em_fr_b1_rosie_nd&recur_weekly=on&recurring=on&amount=10")

Rosie Castro

body:
| | Friend --
[](http://my.yang2020.com/?e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=04232019_newsletter&n=1) Hello, I hope you are doing great! I am writing this from LA, where we met with local community leaders and then proceeded to have one of our biggest rallies yet. 2,000+ people came out to celebrate our message, and we made [the local LA news](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=rjVzrvzZYHqQ6veXrQNXoRe798vR8dRX0hs3tW8ocNiI7kT_AGUYIvyiDLpxu_6iwR65n05OnTugDbz_xO5ZMhcxoaAxVFTK23Irw3LrMPDNna4je3mk4CAEF3aYeG_vIRR3yUHIOGfjyuYuVvNvM-FAjZpQFqIhmy64wgfggfk&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=04232019_newsletter&n=2 "Sen. Cory Booker, Andrew Yang Celebrate Earth
Day"). It was a blast. The [Humanity First Tour](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=c5dAo5qulKAncvNYjq2uGcnHAN92nM1CHInwgA-PIi7HdTRyEez5JIs0AF2zI3zA&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=04232019_newsletter&n=3 "Humanity First Tour Schedule") has been a ton of fun. The best thing about our events is the energy and optimism of the people who show up. Plus our playlist gets better all of the time. The press continues to roll in—[538 noted](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=bMa6zTKUWT9D9F3Z4cDiDFUs13zntMl_ML4m7OqiiGEr1PL9ZkvNu4hnhLA3EEKEFaGlyHgWmWZjeum5b-YA-YX4ddgDho0P4-hcDrUbExFPRo4bhA60dv9f1L7Yoyh2&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=04232019_newsletter&n=4 "Who Might Make The Democratic Debate Stage?") that I am one of only 6 candidates to meet both the polling AND donation requirements to make the DNC debates (the others are Buttigieg, Sanders, O'Rourke, Harris, and Warren). Thank you for making it happen. [USA Today had me at #1](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=HVBK5x9dnBx-ujVjYaP8mWVCUJ4GQNcKr10t8dkBw_l9rQ6hDP-nEeI-krAtpEbKMGe7P5Uoj-x6Ma-ohOWzA-kwMm2qGEE_GVUv6mlo1YUIltakQXTIiOigx0ClAmJNX9GvpN3RRTtk3WHZKMCiTXH1TEYXuQRtrSpR5dK9Zuc&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=04232019_newsletter&n=5 "Andrew Yang, Marianne Williamson, Bernie
Sanders are the 2020 candidates standing out to our readers") from their readers in terms of candidates they were excited about. This week we are [heading to Nevada](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=uuUyPGiVHWR_V3cUbcvCWdikqgOEmv95as43Y58cghcsin65DZor0mRB4_mcZi0qSWuGscy5sirVMYSOojCBTpRWKLZSD45CuqzHqmCZOzw&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=04232019_newsletter&n=6 "Andrew Yang's
Humanity First Tour Rally in Las Vegas"), which is the 3rd state to vote for the Democratic nominee. For those of you who aren’t super into politics (which may describe about 90% of you), the order of states voting goes (dates subject to change):
1. Iowa – February 3rd, 2020
2. New Hampshire – February 11th, 2020
3. Nevada – February 22nd, 2020
4. South Carolina – February 29th, 2020
5. California, Texas, Massachusetts, Minnesota, North Carolina, Virginia, Alabama, Arkansas, Oklahoma, Tennessee, Utah, Vermont, Democrats Abroad* – March 3rd, 2020.

This is why you always hear about voters in Iowa and New Hampshire in particular—because they vote first, they have an enormous amount of influence. Historically, you need to finish at the top of the heap in one or both of those states to win the nomination. This is in part because the results in early states drive voters in later states—if you do well in the early states, you get much more support, money, and attention every day. By the time the calendar gets to March, the nomination is often down to 1 or 2 candidates. This time, that may hold true again. But there is some thought that the nomination calendar will stretch through the Spring and Summer in 2020 because the field is so fragmented that it will be hard for any one candidate to achieve a majority. This is particularly true given that superdelegates—who went en masse for Hillary Clinton last time—cannot vote until the 2nd round of voting at the Democratic convention in Milwaukee in July, a change they made to give more power to grassroots voters. Some believe that there will be a contested convention where no one has a majority and there will be a big negotiation to determine the nominee. It is hard to know—a field this big on the Democratic side is unprecedented. We are in uncharted territory. It’s one reason the opportunity for us is so historic. One thing to note about the scenario above—if we get a significant number of delegates, we could be in position to ride into Milwaukee in position to decide the Democratic nominee. Universal Basic Income, updated economic measurements, and a more modern approach to AI as part of the Democratic Party platform? Sounds good to me. It’s very achievable. To get there, we need to win support everywhere, but particularly in the early states. I am excited to campaign in Nevada—[where we already are at 3% support](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=fVc8ucaySv8SFEdMhk87O-J7Kfx-4kCDXGvdbkDZpGHQ86-oQ3ksB4uW4aUMuXoURRL9QRnTB-bcVwtl39Egj1QoHaP_LbUeSPWabiizW4ojdjC_Be88I6Yav5UhlrAkSWpJ9ui9gWibfSXfguKu5ePgxu-ExFxn6YrhJFHqyUDl3Byat2xa0YRKuqhsPd-Zfh8fgF4SL4uBWcBDITl4UA&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=04232019_newsletter&n=7 "Emerson Polling in Nevada")—[this week and Iowa later this week](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=c5dAo5qulKAncvNYjq2uGcnHAN92nM1CHInwgA-PIi7HdTRyEez5JIs0AF2zI3zA&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=04232019_newsletter&n=8 "Andrew Yang's
Humanity First Tour Schedule"). This will be my 11th trip to Iowa and we now have many friends there. I believe that we will outperform any polling in the early states in part because we are attracting folks who will be voting for the first time in a Democratic caucus or primary. But we still have a lot of work to do. The goal is to win. We can do it. History is providing us a golden opportunity. Let’s make the most of it. The next four stops in the [Humanity First Tour](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=c5dAo5qulKAncvNYjq2uGcnHAN92nM1CHInwgA-PIi7HdTRyEez5JIs0AF2zI3zA&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=04232019_newsletter&n=9 "Humanity First Tour Schedule") are [Las Vegas](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=uuUyPGiVHWR_V3cUbcvCWdikqgOEmv95as43Y58cghcsin65DZor0mRB4_mcZi0qSWuGscy5sirVMYSOojCBTpRWKLZSD45CuqzHqmCZOzw&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=04232019_newsletter&n=10 "Andrew Yang's Humanity First Rally in Las
Vegas"), [Des Moines](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=uuUyPGiVHWR_V3cUbcvCWWmOECFGhvzg9qgv352fDgfL-8v9exXDkBe47jS4Re_-PoVa7CjI-58bE-KieStvUYJQI9PqpKaGRIOjDOWfPgHjVk0zE_wggOm9YGMDVS92wBbrnlSV9SXtU5pEbJ7zHg&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=04232019_newsletter&n=11 "Andrew Yang's Humanity First Rally in Des Moines"), [Seattle](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=uuUyPGiVHWR_V3cUbcvCWdikqgOEmv95as43Y58cghcUJkVmfetEAp2sK9sThwBXl6kF9b220zNTt1ymCeJCsZOe56OrYKLWUp764mKCE5A&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=04232019_newsletter&n=12 "Andrew Yang's Humanity First Rally in Seattle"), and [Minneapolis](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=uuUyPGiVHWR_V3cUbcvCWdikqgOEmv95as43Y58cghfQNMwNimpI6IcIFBC904TiQMcwbKUEVi49gZIY2_StcdgI8QQPhoX7uuq0QjXCUcw&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=04232019_newsletter&n=13 "Andrew Yang's Humanity First Rally in Minneapolis"), and then back to [New York for a big rally in Washington Square Park](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=uuUyPGiVHWR_V3cUbcvCWdikqgOEmv95as43Y58cghf92CPOI7pOoedoIqdOGAc6xUoSty6OWRxsrXn5Uz0RNzDr1f5Qjsj1hZQfbdRmW3g&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=04232019_newsletter&n=14 "Andrew Yang's Humanity First Rally in

#
[**Contribute $5 now to send Betsy Dirksen Londrigan to Congress in 2020.**](http://action.dickdurbin. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dd-londrigan?refcode=em190423&refcode2=Graphic1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&addr2=&city=Englewood&state=FL&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: Russia
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand 2020
body:
I just became the first 2020 candidate to offer a written cybersecurity pledge:
| | |
---
| | "There's nothing wrong with taking information from Russians."

_-- Rudy Giuliani
Personal lawyer to President Trump
April 21, 2019_
---
Peter,

Russia attacked our democracy in 2016. And they're going to try to do it again.

That's why I just became the first 2020 candidate to offer a written cybersecurity pledge: I will not seek, use or weaponize stolen or hacked materials in this presidential campaign.

**Every other presidential candidate should join me. Nothing short of the bedrock of our democracy is at stake.**
**[Add your name to call on the rest of the field to sign my 2020 Cybersecurity Pledge and promise not to use hacked or stolen materials in this campaign. Together, we can send a clear message to those who seek to harm our democracy--at home and abroad.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/s/add-your-name-defend-democracy?firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&source=jtk3067-ja-gfp-nd&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fkg-2020-cybersecurity-pledge%3Frefcode%3Djtk3067-ja-gfp-nd")**
[](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/s/add-your-name-defend-democracy?firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&source=jtk3067-ja-gfp-nd&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fkg-2020-cybersecurity-pledge%3Frefcode%3Djtk3067-ja-gfp-nd")
Russia is a foreign adversary, and their interference in our 2016 election was a direct attack on our democracy. So it's inconceivable that Trump and his top aides are normalizing the idea of "taking information from Russians" for political gain.

We MUST protect our elections from foreign interference if we want to protect our democracy. That starts with pledging to not use any stolen or hacked materials for political gain in the primary or general election this cycle.

I know politics are tough, but we can all agree we have to prioritize protecting our democracy over scoring cheap hits to win an election.
The entire 2020 field must join me in taking this crucial pledge, and promise not to use any hacked or stolen materials.

**Show your support for my 2020 Cybersecurity Pledge and add your name to call on every other presidential candidate to promise not to use hacked or stolen materials in this campaign:**
**[https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/s/add-your-name-defend-democracy](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. Thank you,

Kirsten

subject: Will you?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROMANOFF FOR COLORADO
body:
The high-school student I met on the Western Slope last week had a personal reason for asking: both his parents faced a substance use disorder. Like an estimated 67,000 other Coloradans, they didn't g | [ ](https://secure.andrewromanoff.com/page/m/7a984ad5/33d1dea4/3b739046/3a3a6e7d/1106647178/VEsH/ "http://www.andrewromanoff.com")
---
| | | "Will you do something about drug addiction?" The high-school student I met on the Western Slope last week had a personal reason for asking: both his parents faced a substance use disorder. Like an estimated 67,000 other Coloradans, they didn't get treatment. **I led the fight for the treatment of mental health and substance use disorders in the state legislature. I 'll do the same--[with your help](https://secure.andrewromanoff.com/page/m/7a984ad5/33d1dea4/3b739046/3a3a6e7c/1106647178/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/romanoff2020")--in the U.S. Senate.** As president of Mental Health Colorado, I spent the last four years working to improve access to care--and reduce our reliance on the criminal justice system. My colleagues and I helped secure more than $68 million per year in life-saving services for families in need. But such services are still too scarce, especially in rural communities. Stigma and discrimination stop millions of Americans from seeking mental health care or drug treatment in the first place. **[Will you help me ensure that all Americans get the care they need?](https://secure.andrewromanoff.com/page/m/7a984ad5/33d1dea4/3b739046/3a3a6e7c/1106647178/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/romanoff2020")** My family and I know what can happen when a mental health or substance use disorder goes untreated. As Colorado's next senator, I'll fight to **strengthen insurance coverage, protect Medicaid funding, reduce the cost of care, expand the supply of mental health and substance use professionals, and put a greater priority on prevention and early intervention.** No one should end up in prison or die on account of an illness we can treat. **[This cause needs champions](https://secure.andrewromanoff.com/page/m/7a984ad5/33d1dea4/3b739046/3a3a6e7c/1106647178/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/romanoff2020")** --that's what you and I can be. Andrew Romanoff

subject: We have a bit of a problem our hands
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bernie 2020
body:
Here’s the issue we’re facing:
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=1132%2E150750%2EH_v8Ud)

Friends -
Our campaign has a bit of a problem on our hands, and we were hoping you might help.
The good news is, we have **_far more**_ individual donations to our campaign than anyone else. At more than 1 million, we’re about three times ahead of anyone else.
The bad news is, our average donation amount is **_far less**_ than anyone else. This month our average donation is lower than it’s ever been – right around $14.
That means it takes 200 donations to match 1 max-out check being scooped up at a swanky fundraiser held by one of our opponents.
So we have two choices: we need more donations or we need people to give **_just a bit more**_ than they have before.
Right now, most first-time donors are giving three dollars. If you could possibly chip in $10, we’d be back on track to hold our fundraising advantage. And that’s important, because if we do, we’ll win. What do you say?
[**Can you make a donation to our campaign today? Help us hold our fundraising advantage in this race. Combine that with our organizing and enthusiasm advantage, and we’re going to win.**](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/10584?t=3&akid=1132%2E150750%2EH_v8Ud)
Thank you very much for everything you do to power this campaign. We are doing something totally unique in the way this effort is funded — something that will change the future of American politics. And that’s only possible because of people like you.
All my best,
Faiz Shakir
Campaign Manager

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Inside look at Ivanka Trump’s 4-day Africa trip
body:
Last week, Advisor to the President Ivanka Trump visited Ethiopia and Ivory Coast as part of a major push for women’s economic empowerment... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f93a66ec36&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • April 23, 2019
---
| | |
# **Inside look at Ivanka Trump’s 4-day Africa trip**

**Last week,** Advisor to the President Ivanka Trump visited Ethiopia and Ivory Coast as part of a major push for women’s economic empowerment across the developing world.

**Two months ago,** the Trump Administration launched the Women’s Global Development and Prosperity Initiative, or [W-GDP](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=af6337dfb3&e=3ee4985eea), in a first-of-its-kind move to boost women’s participation and advancement in the global workforce. W-GDP seeks to reach 50 million women in the developing world by 2025.

**“The economic empowerment** of women shouldn’t be viewed as a ‘women’s issue,’” [Ms. Trump wrote then](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9cf30f42e0&e=3ee4985eea). “By investing in women, we are investing in a future in which countries can support themselves by unleashing the potential of their own people.”
🎬**Watch:** [Ivanka Trump’s visit to Ethiopia](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2487852202&e=3ee4985eea)
**In Ethiopia,** Ms. Trump addressed female leaders and entrepreneurs at the United Nations Economic Commission for Africa, where she announced the launch of OPIC 2X Africa. In partnership with the Overseas Private Investment Corporation, this W-GDP initiative will help mobilize $1 billion to support projects led by women in Sub-Saharan Africa.

**Part two** of the trip took her to Ivory Coast, where the U.S. delegation announced a $2 million commitment to women workers in the cocoa industry. The investment will open more doors for female farmers by increasing access to capital and training programs. Ms. Trump met some of these entrepreneurs [at a local cocoa farm](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ad3953793b&e=3ee4985eea).

**Before heading home,** Ms. Trump delivered a keynote address at the Women Entrepreneurs Finance Initiative (We-Fi) Summit. During President Trump’s first year in office, America became a founding member of We-Fi, and in the organization’s first year, it allocated $120 million toward projects that help female entrepreneurs globally.

**“Societies that empower women** to succeed are more peaceful and more prosperous. And this is evident in all the research that we see,” Ms. Trump says.

**[A closer look inside Ivanka Trump’s trip to Ivory Coast](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=225a6e3770&e=3ee4985eea)**
---
|
---
|
## **Add $60+ billion for America under President Trump’s new trade deal**

**One of President Trump’s** signature promises was to get America out of bad trade deals—and negotiate brand-new ones that finally benefit our great workers. Late last year, the President delivered on that pledge in a big way when he signed the U.S.–Mexico–Canada Trade Agreement (USCMA), replacing the outdated NAFTA.

**The USMCA** will make life better for American workers by “bringing back our manufacturing jobs, expanding American agriculture, protecting intellectual property, and ensuring that more cars are proudly stamped with four beautiful words: made in the USA,” President Trump said during his second State of the Union Address.

**A new report** from the International Trade Commission backs the President up, explaining that the new agreement will add $68.2 billion to America’s economy while creating 176,000 new jobs. “The agreement would likely have a positive impact on all broad industry sectors within the U.S. economy,” the report says. “Manufacturing would experience the largest percentage gains in output, exports, wages, and employment.”

**The evidence** is quickly making clear that USMCA will offer a bright future for the U.S. economy once Congress enacts it. The question now: How much longer will Washington make our workers wait?

**[Headlines: USMCA will boost economic growth and create jobs](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f6e4eec24a&e=3ee4985eea)**
---
|
---
|
## **Video of the day: Ivanka Trump in Ivory Coast**
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8db7c0a39c&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **“Wonderful and productive** visit to Côte d'Ivoire!” Ivanka Trump tweeted last week as she returned home from a four-day trip to Africa, where she promoted women’s economic empowerment in the developing world.

clean_body: Last week, Advisor to the President Ivanka Trump visited Ethiopia and Ivory Coast as part of a major push for women’s economic empowerment... | | | | | The White House • April 23, 2019 | | | Inside look at Ivanka Trump’s 4-day Africa trip Last week, Advisor to the President Ivanka Trump visited Ethiopia and Ivory Coast as part of a major push for women’s economic empowerment across the developing world. Two months ago, the Trump Administration launched the Women’s Global Development and Prosperity Initiative, or , in a first-of-its-kind move to boost women’s participation and advancement in the global workforce. W-GDP seeks to reach 50 million women in the developing world by 2025. “The economic empowerment of women shouldn’t be viewed as a ‘women’s issue,’” . “By investing in women, we are investing in a future in which countries can support themselves by unleashing the potential of their own people.” 🎬Watch: In Ethiopia, Ms. Trump addressed female leaders and entrepreneurs at the United Nations Economic Commission for Africa, where she announced the launch of OPIC 2X Africa. In partnership with the Overseas Private Investment Corporation, this W-GDP initiative will help mobilize $1 billion to support projects led by women in Sub-Saharan Africa. Part two of the trip took her to Ivory Coast, where the U.S. delegation announced a $2 million commitment to women workers in the cocoa industry. The investment will open more doors for female farmers by increasing access to capital and training programs. Ms. Trump met some of these entrepreneurs . Before heading home, Ms. Trump delivered a keynote address at the Women Entrepreneurs Finance Initiative (We-Fi) Summit. During President Trump’s first year in office, America became a founding member of We-Fi, and in the organization’s first year, it allocated $120 million toward projects that help female entrepreneurs globally. “Societies that empower women to succeed are more peaceful and more prosperous. And this is evident in all the research that we see,” Ms. Trump says. | | Add $60+ billion for America under President Trump’s new trade deal One of President Trump’s signature promises was to get America out of bad trade deals—and negotiate brand-new ones that finally benefit our great workers. Late last year, the President delivered on that pledge in a big way when he signed the U.S.–Mexico–Canada Trade Agreement (USCMA), replacing the outdated NAFTA. The USMCA will make life better for American workers by “bringing back our manufacturing jobs, expanding American agriculture, protecting intellectual property, and ensuring that more cars are proudly stamped with four beautiful words: made in the USA,” President Trump said during his second State of the Union Address. A new report from the International Trade Commission backs the President up, explaining that the new agreement will add $68.2 billion to America’s economy while creating 176,000 new jobs. “The agreement would likely have a positive impact on all broad industry sectors within the U.S. economy,” the report says. “Manufacturing would experience the largest percentage gains in output, exports, wages, and employment.” The evidence is quickly making clear that USMCA will offer a bright future for the U.S. economy once Congress enacts it. The question now: How much longer will Washington make our workers wait? | | Video of the day: Ivanka Trump in Ivory Coast | | “Wonderful and productive visit to Côte d'Ivoire!” Ivanka Trump tweeted last week as she returned home from a four-day trip to Africa, where she promoted women’s economic empowerment in the developing world. | | | | | | The White House 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111
